title: Developer Experience Engineering
description: Developer Experience (DX) engineering focuses on reducing friction in the inner development loop through internal tooling, automation, and data-driven improvements.

sections.title: Internal CLI Tooling
sections.description:
Internal CLIs wrap common workflows into a single binary. Build with Go or Rust for cross-platform distribution. Common commands include: pr create (standardized PR template), cf branch (create branch with ticket number), ci status (check CI across all repos), and env list (show all environments). Distribute via GitHub Releases and update via gh extension upgrade --all.

gh extension install org/gh-mycompany
gh mycompany pr create --type fix --scope auth
gh mycompany ci status --watch

sections.title: gh Extensions
sections.description:
GitHub CLI extensions extend gh with org-specific commands. Create an extension by publishing a repo named gh-NAME with an executable of the same name. Use Go scripts for performance or shell scripts for simplicity. Extensions have access to GH_HOST, GH_TOKEN, and gh api context. Publish with gh extension create.

#!/bin/bash
# gh-mycompany
set -e
echo "Fetching team PRs..."
gh search prs --review-requested=@me --state=open \
  --json=title,url,repository --jq '.[].title'

sections.title: DORA Metrics
sections.description:
DORA (DevOps Research & Assessment) metrics measure delivery performance: Deployment Frequency (how often), Lead Time for Change (commit to deploy), Mean Time to Restore (MTTR), and Change Failure Rate (CFR). Collect metrics via the GitHub API: deployments, check runs, and issues. Build dashboards with Datadog, Grafana, or a custom vue. Target elite performance: multiple deploys/day, LT <1 hour, MTTR <1 hour, CFR <5%.

# Query deployment frequency
graphql query '{
  repository(owner:"org", name:"repo") {
    deployments(last:100) {
      nodes { createdAt, updatedAt }
    }
  }
}'

sections.title: Productivity Measurement
sections.description:
Developer productivity is multi-dimensional: satisfaction (e.g., DX survey), efficiency (time to complete tasks), effectiveness (value delivered), and quality (defect rate). Use the SPACE framework (Satisfaction, Performance, Activity, Communication, Efficiency). Measure cycle time from issue creation to deployment. Track WIP limits, PR review turnaround, and context switching via Git log analysis.

# PR cycle time
gh pr view $PR --json=createdAt,mergedAt,closedAt \
  --template '{{.createdAt}} -> {{.mergedAt}}'

sections.title: DX Culture
sections.description: Building DX culture requires dedicated investment and empathy. Establish a DX team or guild with rotating members. Run regular developer surveys (quarterly) and follow up on pain points. Fame internal tooling wins in all-hands. Dogfood tooling before shipping — every DX tool should be used by the DX team first. Track tool adoption and time saved as key metrics.

details: Developer Experience engineering treats the development workflow as a product, with developers as customers. The inner development loop — code, build, test, debug — is the highest-leverage area for DX investment. Every second saved in the inner loop compounds across the entire engineering org. Internal CLIs reduce cognitive load by wrapping complex Git/API workflows into opinionated, discoverable commands. The gh extension ecosystem enables these CLIs to leverage existing auth context and API access. DORA metrics provide a quantitative baseline for DX improvements. The four DORA metrics decompose into deploy frequency (CI pipeline speed + release automation), lead time (merge to production), MTTR (observability + rollback capability), and CFR (testing + code review quality). Elite performers deploy on demand with lead times under one hour. Measurement must be transparent and blame-free — use aggregated data for team-level trends, not individual performance. For productivity measurement, the SPACE framework acknowledges that no single metric captures developer productivity. Combine quantitative (cycle time, build time) with qualitative (satisfaction surveys, NPS). Track the outcomes that matter: time to onboard a new engineer, time to create and merge a PR, and time to resolve an incident. DX culture is maintained through tooling (automating the tedious), empathy (listening to developer feedback), and advocacy (celebrating efficiency wins).
cheatSheet:
Core Practices

  • gh extensions — wrap org-specific workflows into discoverable CLI commands (gh mycompany pr create).
  • DORA metrics — Deployment Frequency, Lead Time, MTTR, Change Failure Rate.
  • SPACE framework — measure Satisfaction, Performance, Activity, Communication, Efficiency.

Staff+ Patterns

  • Treat the development workflow as a product — developers are your customers. Every second saved compounds across the org.
  • Build internal CLIs that wrap complex Git/API workflows into opinionated commands. Distribute via gh extension.
  • Measure cycle time from issue creation to deployment. Track PR review turnaround and WIP limits.

Watch Outs

  • DX metrics can become a blame tool if not aggregated anonymously at team level. Keep measurement transparent and blameless.
  • No single metric captures productivity — combine quantitative (DORA) with qualitative (surveys, NPS).
  • Dogfood tooling before shipping — if your DX team won't use it, neither will anyone else.

Interview Angle

Microsoft/Stripe L6: "Design a developer productivity measurement system for 5K engineers." Discuss: SPACE framework, DORA dashboards per team, quarterly developer surveys, and automated tooling to track cycle time, build time, and onboarding time.
